now.text: Currently investigating molecular biomarkers of olfactory dysfunction in early-stage Parkinson's disease — integrating clinical and transcriptomic data.

summary: Md Zainul Ali is a doctoral research scholar in the Department of Biotechnology & Bioinformatics, School of Life Sciences, University of Hyderabad. His research focuses on the computational exploration of neurodegenerative disorders, with a specialization in Parkinson's disease. He applies machine learning, statistical modeling, and systems biology approaches to identify clinically relevant non-motor features and their molecular underpinnings.

milestones.title: Completed B.Sc. in Biochemistry
milestones.description: Graduated from Aligarh Muslim University with a 7.6 CGPA.

milestones.title: Admitted to M.Sc. Biotechnology
milestones.description: Joined the School of Life Sciences, University of Hyderabad.

milestones.title: Completed M.Sc. with distinction
milestones.description: Graduated with 8.4 CGPA; began PhD groundwork in neurodegeneration.

milestones.title: Started PhD in Bioinformatics
milestones.description: Research scholar in the Department of Biotechnology & Bioinformatics, focusing on computational Parkinson's research.

milestones.title: First peer-reviewed publication
milestones.description: Review article in Neurochemistry International on oxidative phosphorylation in Parkinson's pathogenesis.

milestones.title: First international conference poster

milestones.title: First international oral presentation

experience.description: Computational research on Parkinson's disease, applying machine learning and systems biology approaches to identify clinically relevant biomarkers.
achievements: Developed interactive computational tools for Parkinson's risk assessment
Oral presentation at MDS International Congress (Philadelphia, 2024)
Published review article in Neurochemistry International (2022)

experience.description: Analysis of Complex-I associated genes in Parkinson's disease for risk assessment.
achievements: Integrated Complex-I associated gene interactions into a unified platform
Developed a predictive model for Parkinson's disease risk
Demonstrated compensatory effects among Parkinson's risk genes

projects.title: Early Biomarkers for Parkinson's via ML and WGCNA
projects.subtitle: Molecular basis of smell loss in Parkinson's disease
projects.description: Applying machine learning and WGCNA on clinical and transcriptomic data to detect early biomarkers, with special focus on smell loss; improving patient–healthy classification.
features: A subset of non-motor symptoms predicts Parkinson's disease
Smell loss is the strongest predictive symptom for PD
Integration of clinical and transcriptomic data

projects.title: PD-INSPECT
projects.subtitle: Parkinson's Disease - Integrated Non-motor Symptom-based Prediction and Evaluation using Computational Tool
projects.description: PD-INSPECT integrates transfer learning and collaborative filtering to evaluate Parkinson's disease risk from non-motor symptoms and molecular biomarkers, providing clinician-friendly insights for early diagnosis.
features: Risk assessment based on non-motor symptoms

projects.title: Complex-I Associated Genes in Parkinson's Disease
projects.subtitle: Interactive data exploration
projects.description: An online predictor built with HTML/CSS/JavaScript for gene-based risk assessment in Parkinson's disease.

contact.availability: Open to research collaborations and academic opportunities

conferences.description: Showcased research on network-based analysis of neurodegenerative disorders using multi-omics data integration.

conferences.description: Presented work on machine learning approaches for predicting disease progression in Parkinson's patients.

workshops.title: International Workshop on Computational Biology
workshops.description: Intensive workshop on applying machine learning techniques to biological data analysis and interpretation.

workshops.title: Advanced Bioinformatics Workshop
workshops.description: Hands-on training in computational modeling of biological systems and network analysis techniques.

articles.title: Oxidative phosphorylation–mediated pathogenesis of Parkinson's disease and its implication via Akt signaling
articles.summary: Comprehensive review examining the role of oxidative phosphorylation dysfunction in Parkinson's disease pathogenesis and its implications through Akt signaling pathways.

articles.title: Early Diagnosis of Parkinson's Disease: Utility of Animal Models
articles.summary: Chapter on the utility of animal models in early diagnosis of Parkinson's disease, exploring their role in understanding disease mechanisms and developing diagnostic strategies.
articles.venue: Parkinson's Disease — Animal Models, Current Therapies and Clinical Trials (IntechOpen)

articles.title: Integrating Non-Motor Symptoms and Gender Variability in Machine Learning for Early Parkinson's Disease Detection
articles.summary: Novel machine learning approaches integrating non-motor symptoms and gender variability for early detection of Parkinson's disease.
articles.venue: International Conference on Parkinson's Disease Research, Philadelphia

articles.title: Gender-Specific Classification Models for Parkinson's Disease Using Non-Motor Symptoms and DNA Methylation Data
articles.summary: Gender-specific classification models using non-motor symptoms and DNA methylation data for Parkinson's disease diagnosis.
articles.venue: European Conference on Computational Biology, Copenhagen

seo.metaTitle: Md Zainul Ali — Doctoral Research Scholar, Computational Neuroscience
seo.metaDescription: Md Zainul Ali is a doctoral research scholar specializing in computational approaches to neurodegenerative disorders, with a focus on Parkinson's disease at University of Hyderabad.
